mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting conditions and supports equity multiples, so a sustained decline should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after months with negative FEDFUNDS changes than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year window, reject this signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should depress equity prices over the next month as earnings risk is repriced downward.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not deliver lower median 20-day SPX returns than untriggered observations, or if positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend indicates improving industrial output and cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week through faster growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly-to-monthly INDPRO changes do not correspond to above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal's directional hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combined improvement in RSAFS and HOUST identifies broad demand acceleration: stronger retail sales and homebuilding imply better revenue growth, so the S&P 500 should outperform over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-RSAFS/high-HOUST state does not outperform the rest of the sample on 60-day forward returns, or if either proxy alone fully explains the effect with no incremental interaction lift.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains that coincide with a falling unemployment rate indicate broad labor re-absorption after a shock, which should support equity returns over the next month through stronger consumption and lower default risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down regime does not produce meaningfully higher 20-day SPX returns than single-variable labor improvements, or if the sign flips when payroll gains are broad-based.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation prints are accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound, real-rate expectations become more negative and equities should rerate upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if inflation acceleration is not followed by higher SPX returns after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP contraction or weak quarterly GDPC1 growth signals a demand shortfall that should lower S&P 500 returns over the following quarter by reducing earnings expectations and risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 0 on latest quarterly release
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth quarters are not associated with below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains negative only in revised data but not in vintages available at decision time.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST captures a demand slowdown in retail and housing at the same time, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as cyclical growth expectations weaken quickly.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls and HOUST falls over the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in retail sales and housing starts do not precede lower 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears once either component is removed.
